title: Skill manifest
description:
Registers a user- or agent-invocable skill with the harness. Skills are named procedures invoked via /<name> from the conversation pane (GUI/TUI) or — when `proactive_trigger` is set — suggested by the model itself. The harness expands the manifest's `prompt_template` (with `${arg}` substitution) into a regular user turn; the §2.5 agent loop runs unchanged. Skill invocations are recorded as a `note` on the next turn's ledger entry.

Stored at `~/.atelier/skills/<name>.json` (global), `<repo>/.atelier/skills/<name>.json` (per-repo, overrides global), or bundled with `atelier-core` (overridable by either). Built-in v1 bundled skills: `/review`, `/security-review`, `/test`. `/help` and `/init` are harness-intercepted CLI verbs, not skill manifests.

name.description: Skill identifier. Invoked as `/<name>` from the conversation pane. Per-repo manifests override same-name global ones; both override bundled defaults of the same name.

description.description: One-line description. Shown in `/help` and the GUI skill list. Used to guide proactive suggestion when `proactive_trigger` is set.

prompt_template.description: Prompt expansion template that becomes the next user turn. May reference declared args via `${arg_name}` substitution. May reference `${repo_root}` and `${atelier_md}` as harness-provided context variables.

default.description: String default used when the arg is omitted. Only valid if `required` is false.

pinned_context.description: File paths (relative to repo root, glob-supported) to pin into the §5 context panel before the prompt fires. Useful for skills that should always see ATELIER.md, a specific test file, etc.

tools_required.description: Tool names this skill expects to be available. If any are missing at invocation time, the UI surfaces a warning and the user can skip / install the tool / cancel.

proactive_trigger.description: Natural-language description of when the model should auto-suggest this skill. When present, the skill's name + this text are summarised in the system prompt so the model is aware of it. When absent, the skill is manual-only and not advertised to the model.

side_effect_class.description: Optional §8 trust-budget hint. If absent, the skill inherits the maximum side-effect class of its constituent tool calls; if no tools fire, defaults to local-safe.
